HandlersTest covers SketchHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, and ExplorationHandlers but has no test methods for CorpusQueryHandlers
The 413 path uses RequestEntityTooLargeException (a new type from rounds 21-22) — this exception type and its mapping have no handler-level test

issues.summary: ConcordanceHandlers relation-not-found fallback (proximity BCQL pattern) is not tested
issues.suggestion: Add a HandlersTest case that passes an unknown relation ID and verifies the response contains 'fallback: true' and a non-empty 'bcql' field constructed from the proximity pattern.
evidence: handleConcordanceExamples: 'if (bcqlQuery == null || bcqlQuery.isEmpty()) { bcqlQuery = String.format(...proximity fallback...); fallback = true; }'
HandlersTest.handleConcordanceExamples tests only the missing-parameter 400 paths — not the fallback=true path
The fallback log.warn is in production code but never verified in tests; if the relation lookup changes, the fallback could silently produce incorrect results

issues.summary: /health endpoint is registered with createContext directly rather than registerGetHandler, so it lacks CORS OPTIONS preflight handling
issues.suggestion: Wrap the /health handler with wrapWithErrorHandling for consistency: 'HttpApiUtils.wrapWithErrorHandling(exchange -> HttpApiUtils.sendJsonResponse(exchange, Collections.singletonMap("status", "ok")), "Health")'. This is low-risk since sendJsonResponse rarely throws, but keeps the pattern uniform.
evidence: registerRoutes(): 'registerGetHandler(server, "/health", exchange -> HttpApiUtils.sendJsonResponse(exchange, ...))' — wait, actually /health uses registerGetHandler in the code — let me note this correctly

Looking at the code: 'registerGetHandler(server, "/health", exchange -> HttpApiUtils.sendJsonResponse(exchange, Collections.singletonMap("status", "ok")));' — /health IS registered via registerGetHandler

So /health does get OPTIONS handling — the concern is that the lambda does not go through wrapWithErrorHandling, so any IOException from sendJsonResponse propagates as an unhandled exception to the JDK server

issues.summary: 'seed' (singular) is used for single-seed endpoints but 'seeds' (plural, comma-separated) is used for multi-seed and comparison, creating a cardinality confusion risk at the call site
issues.suggestion: Document the cardinality rules in the endpoint Javadoc (which already exists) and add explicit validation in handleSemanticFieldExplore to reject inputs that contain commas: 'if (seed.contains(",")) throw new IllegalArgumentException("seed must be a single lemma; use /explore-multi for multiple seeds")'. This turns a silent misuse into a clear 400 error.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: 'HttpApiUtils.requireParam(params, "seed")' — expects a single value
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: 'HttpApiUtils.requireParam(params, "seeds")' — expects comma-separated values
handleSemanticFieldComparison: 'HttpApiUtils.requireParam(params, "seeds")' — expects comma-separated values
handleConcordanceExamples: 'HttpApiUtils.requireParam(params, "seed")' — expects a single value
There is no error if a client passes 'seeds=theory,model' to /explore — it would be accepted as a single seed value 'theory,model', returning an empty result without error

issues.summary: CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin default ('http://localhost:3000') is read from system property without validation, allowing any origin to be injected via -Dcors.allow.origin
issues.suggestion: Add a simple whitelist check or warn on non-localhost origins: 'if (!origin.startsWith("http://localhost") && !origin.startsWith("https://localhost")) { logger.warn("cors.allow.origin is set to a non-localhost value: {}", origin); }'. For stricter control, reject wildcard ('*') origins at startup.
evidence: 'String origin = System.getProperty("cors.allow.origin", DEFAULT_CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN);'
No validation that origin is a well-formed URL or matches a whitelist
An operator could inadvertently set '-Dcors.allow.origin=*' which would allow all origins — contradicting the intent of the loopback-only security model
